---
title: "modelling"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
library(mixOmics)
library(dplyr)
```
# tune<- function( X,Y, ncomp, keepX=all variables, manual=TRUE )
1. how many ncomp? mixomics to perl
2. how many variables to keep for each component?
a. manual
b. automatic - discuss next time if we need it
- decide to use one function or two
# if ncomp> 1 --> manual
# if ncomp =1 --> automatic tuning
```{r}
# [ORIGINAL] run_mint() and plot_mint_results() were defined here during development.
# [UPDATED] Both functions promoted to Function_Helpers.R — available via source("Function_Helpers.R").

run_mint <- function (X, Y, ncomp=2, dist="centroids.dist"){
   tune.comp1 <- tune(method = "mint.splsda",
                      X = X, Y = Y, study = study,
                      ncomp = 1,
                      test.keepX = seq(10, ncol(X), 1),
                      dist = dist)
   # study is the name of study as factors ,Y is the row of diseases df$diseases X is all the studies combined abund

   # tuning component 1
   optimal.comp1 <- tune.comp1$choice.keepX
   cat("Optimal keepX for Component 1:", optimal.comp1, "\n\n")
   plot(tune.comp1, main = "Component 1 Tuning")

   cat("Step 2: Tuning Component 2...\n")
   tune.comp2 <- tune(method = "mint.splsda",
                      X = X, Y = Y, study = study,
                      ncomp = 2,
                      test.keepX = seq(10, ncol(X), 1),
                      already.tested.X = optimal.comp1,
                      dist = dist)

   optimal.comp2 <- tune.comp2$choice.keepX[2]
   cat("Optimal keepX for Component 2:", optimal.comp2, "\n\n")
   plot(tune.comp2, main = "Component 2 Tuning")

   optimal.keepX <- c(optimal.comp1, optimal.comp2)

   cat("Step 3: Building final model...\n")
   cat("Using keepX =", paste(optimal.keepX, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

   model <- mint.splsda(X = X, Y = Y, study = study,
                        ncomp = ncomp,
                        keepX = optimal.keepX)

   # STEP 4: EVALUATE MODEL
   cat("Step 4: Evaluating model...\n")
   perf <- perf(model, validation="Mfolds", folds=5, nrepeat=10, dist="centroids.dist")

   cat("\nBalanced Error Rate (BER):\n")
   print(perf$global.error$BER)

   cat("\nError Rate per Class:\n")
   print(perf$global.error$error.rate.class)

   selected.comp1 = selectVar(model, comp = 1)$name
   selected.comp2 = selectVar(model, comp = 2)$name
   ttl_taxa = tolower(unique(c(selected.comp1, selected.comp2)))

   results <- list(
     model = model,
     optimal.keepX = optimal.keepX,
     performance = perf,
     selected.features.comp1 = selected.comp1,
     selected.features.comp2 = selected.comp2,
     features.all = ttl_taxa
   )
   return(results)
 }


 plot_mint_results <- function(results, Y, study) {
   model <- results$model

   # Score plot
   plotIndiv(model,
             group = Y,
             pch = as.numeric(factor(study)) + 13,
             pch.levels = study,
             title = 'MINT sPLS-DA',
             legend = TRUE,
             legend.title = 'Disease',
             legend.title.pch = 'Study',
             ellipse = TRUE)

   # Loadings - Component 1
   plotLoadings(model, comp = 1, legend = TRUE, contrib = 'max',
                title = 'Feature Loadings - Component 1', method = 'median', ndisplay = 20)
   legend("topright", legend = levels(Y), fill = c("orange", "blue"))

   # Loadings - Component 2
   plotLoadings(model, comp = 2, legend = TRUE, contrib = 'max',
                title = 'Feature Loadings - Component 2', method = 'median', ndisplay = 20)
   legend("topright", legend = levels(Y), fill = c("orange", "blue"))
 }
```

```{r}
## testing code
# ============================================================================
# PREPARE DATA FROM NAMED LIST
# ============================================================================

# Assuming df is your named list with structure:
# df$study1$abund (matrix with rownames = SID, colnames = taxa)
# df$study1$meta (data frame with rownames = SID, columns = disease, gender, etc.)
# df$study2$abund, df$study2$meta, etc.

# Step 1: Combine abundance data from all studies
X <- bind_rows(lapply(names(df), function(study_name) {
  as.data.frame(df[[study_name]]$abund)
})) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Step 2: Combine metadata from all studies
meta_combined <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(df), function(study_name) {
  df[[study_name]]$meta
}))

# Step 3: Create study factor (repeats study name for each sample)
study <- factor(rep(names(df), times = sapply(df, function(x) nrow(x$abund))))
# Step 4: Extract disease variable
Y <- meta_combined$disease

# Verify everything matches
cat("X dimensions:", nrow(X), "samples x", ncol(X), "taxa\n")
cat("Y length:", length(Y), "\n")
cat("study length:", length(study), "\n")
cat("All rownames match:", all(rownames(X) == rownames(meta_combined)), "\n")
cat("Study factor levels:", levels(study), "\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# RUN THE PIPELINE
# ============================================================================

results <- run_mint(X = X, Y = Y, ncomp = 2, dist = "centroids.dist")

# ============================================================================
# VIEW RESULTS
# ============================================================================

cat("\n\nRESULTS SUMMARY:\n")
cat("Optimal keepX:", results$optimal.keepX, "\n")
cat("Selected taxa (Component 1):", length(results$selected.features.comp1), "\n")
cat("Selected taxa (Component 2):", length(results$selected.features.comp2), "\n")
cat("All unique taxa:", length(results$features.all), "\n")
cat("\nSelected taxa:\n")
print(results$features.all)

meta_combined <- bind_rows(lapply(names(df), function(study_name) {
  as.data.frame(df[[study_name]]$meta)
}))

study <- meta_combined$study

plot_mint_results(results, Y, study)
```
